symptoms.symptom_name: Acute unilateral (right) calf swelling with marked circumference discrepancy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute unilateral calf swelling with a >3 cm discrepancy is a key Wells criterion strongly supporting DVT.
Swelling occurs in both, so this feature alone does not discriminate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep dull aching calf pain/tenderness with a palpable tender venous cord

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised deep venous tenderness along a palpable cord supports thrombosis of the deep venous system.

symptoms.symptom_name: Warmth and erythema over the affected calf with superficial collateral vein distension

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Warmth, erythema and collateral vein distension accompany venous outflow obstruction in DVT.
Warmth and erythema also occur in cellulitis; discrimination requires assessment of skin breaks and margins.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent prolonged immobility - 14-hour long-haul flight three days before onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recent immobility is a major provoking risk factor and a Wells criterion raising DVT probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, breathlessness or haemoptysis (no current PE)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of respiratory symptoms makes current PE unlikely, but does not remove the ongoing risk requiring safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: No anticoagulant/oral contraceptive use and no skin break at the site

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a skin break or portal of entry lowers the likelihood of cellulitis relative to DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-compressible deep vein on compression duplex ultrasonography

investigation_details.investigation_name: Compression duplex ultrasound of the lower limb
investigation_details.expected_result: Non-compressible deep vein confirming thrombus in the deep venous system
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: DVT remains unconfirmed and anticoagulation cannot be safely rationalised, while thrombus propagation/PE risk continues.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A non-compressible vein on compression ultrasound is the confirmatory diagnostic finding for DVT.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acute unilateral calf swelling with marked circumference discrepancy, deep venous tenderness/cord, warmth/erythema, and recent long-haul immobility together give a high Wells probability of DVT, mandating urgent ultrasound and anticoagulation with PE safety-netting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Compression duplex ultrasound of the lower limb
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line confirmatory test for DVT (non-compressible vein); should be fast-tracked, ideally same day or within the pathway timeframe.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline bloods (FBC, renal function, coagulation) prior to anticoagulation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Needed to assess bleeding risk and dose anticoagulation safely.
Of limited value when clinical (Wells) probability is already high - imaging takes precedence; a negative D-dimer would not exclude DVT in a high-probability patient.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Reassurance and rest at home without imaging or anticoagulation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Given the high Wells probability and PE risk, deferring assessment risks thrombus propagation and fatal embolism.
